title: Failure means not fitting in — frequency-tribes thesis (2022)
html:
Failure means not fitting in — frequency-tribes thesis (2022)

Source: 2022-01-07 Apple Note. New-year reflection on fitting in as the social-failure axis, with a frequency-tribes resolution.

TL;DR

  • "Failure is terrifying and humiliating. It means not fitting in" — defines failure socially, not by metrics. Earliest-version of founder loneliness arc.
  • "Fitting in isn't as nice" — counter-move; the act of fitting in itself loses appeal once examined.
  • "Always will be a place where you fit in, always never likely feels right. Like a massive bass behind you thumping" — the frequency-tribes thesis: somewhere your tribe exists (Silicon Valley, jail, a niche subculture); the search-cost is finding the right vibration.
  • "My theory is they do, and they have searched with people to whom they are on the same frequency with" — operational: outsiders aren't broken, they're under-routed. Same instinct as mentors-from-afar.
  • OSU sophomore winter mind-state: lonely-ish, intellectualizing the loneliness. This is the proto-version of the founder-loneliness articulated more sharply in 2025-2026 notes.

Connections

  • founder loneliness 2022 2026 — synthesis target
  • magnetic aura
  • osu
  • soul as beacon
